keyPointsSummary: 'Would like to' is the polite/formal version of 'want to'. Use it in interviews, office, restaurants, and formal conversations.
Formula: Subject + would like to + base verb (e.g., I would like to meet you.)
Contraction: I'd / You'd / He'd / She'd / We'd / They'd + like to + base verb (e.g., I'd like to know more.)
Negative: Subject + would not like to / wouldn't like to + base verb (e.g., She wouldn't like to work late.)
Question: Would + subject + like to + base verb? (e.g., Would you like to have coffee?)
Short answers: Yes, I would. / No, I wouldn't. (NOT: Yes, I would like. / No, I would not like.)
'Would like to' is NEVER followed by -ing form. WRONG: I would like eating. RIGHT: I would like to eat.
After 'would', always use BASE FORM 'like' — never 'likes'. WRONG: She would likes. RIGHT: She would like.
For questions, use 'Would', NOT 'Does/Do'. WRONG: Does she would like to...? RIGHT: Would she like to...?
Politeness scale: want to (casual) < would like to (polite) < would love to (enthusiastic)
'Would like + noun' (without 'to') is also possible: I would like a glass of water. (verb 'drink/have' is implied)
In indirect speech: He said he would like to meet the CEO.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं एक कप चाय लेना चाहूँगा।
quickQuiz.answer: I would like to have a cup of tea.
Would you like to see the menu?
She would not like to work overtime.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम आपको यह position offer करना चाहेंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: We would like to offer you this position.
I would like to introduce myself.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या वह इस project में शामिल होना चाहेगा?
quickQuiz.answer: Would he like to join this project?

quickQuiz.hindi: वे contract cancel नहीं करना चाहेंगे।
quickQuiz.answer: They would not like to cancel the contract.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं अपनी communication skills improve करना चाहूँगा।
quickQuiz.answer: I would like to improve my communication skills.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या आप हमारे office का tour लेना चाहेंगे?
quickQuiz.answer: Would you like to take a tour of our office?

quickQuiz.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह director से मिलना चाहेगा।
quickQuiz.answer: He said he would like to meet the director.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं एक savings account खोलना चाहूँगा।
quickQuiz.answer: I would like to open a savings account.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या आप कुछ और जानना चाहेंगे?
quickQuiz.answer: Would you like to know anything else?

mustRememberRules: RULE 1: 'Would like to' + BASE VERB (never + ing form). I would like TO EAT (NOT: I would like eating).
RULE 2: 'Would' does not change for any subject. I/He/She/We/They — all use 'would like to'.
RULE 3: For questions: Would + subject + like to + base verb? (NOT: Do/Does + subject + would like?)
RULE 4: For negatives: Subject + would not / wouldn't + like to + base verb.
RULE 5: Short answers: Yes, I would. / No, I wouldn't. (Keep it short and natural.)
RULE 6: Contraction 'I'd like to' sounds more natural in speech. Use it confidently.
RULE 7: After 'would', the verb STAYS in base form — 'like', NOT 'likes' or 'liked'.
RULE 8: In professional/formal English, always prefer 'would like to' over just 'want to'.
